title: excoecaria canjoerensis
intro: Excoecaria canjoerensis, also known as the poison tree or the poison wood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. Traditionally, the leaves and sap of the plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Excoecaria canjoerensis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural antiseptic. Excoecaria canjoerensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it has also been used in traditional remedies for its analgesic and antiparasitic properties.
